rationale: The citing paper gives Lambrecht and Tucker (2013) a distinct role by summarizing its core finding that dynamic versus generic retargeting effectiveness depends on browsing signals tied to preference refinement. It then explicitly uses that finding to motivate its own objective of studying how display-ad effects differ across purchase-funnel stages. The citation is part of the conceptual setup and contribution positioning, not a generic literature-list mention.
relation_description: Hoban and Bucklin build on Lambrecht and Tucker's stage-contingent view of online ad effectiveness. They extend it by testing stage-specific effects of display advertising more broadly across multiple funnel stages using a randomized field experiment and response modeling.
